reasoning:
The 30-day average of fatalities over the preceding 360 days is approx 325.5. This average covers roughly early 2025 to early 2026. During 2025, violence in Ecuador increased significantly over the previous year, reaching an all-time high of ~9,100 homicides, up from ~6,964 in 2024. Because violence was trending upward throughout the year (with late 2025 implied homicide rates of ~773/month compared to ~755/month in early-mid 2025), the 360-day average is pulled down by the earlier, slightly less violent months. Therefore, the expected value for a 30-day period at the very end of this timeframe (late Dec 2025 to Feb 2026) is naturally higher than the 360-day average. Furthermore, January 2026 reports from ACLED specifically cite a "renewed surge" and intensified turf wars in Machala prison, as well as continued high national homicide counts. This strongly implies the short-term trailing 30 days will exceed 325.5. 

For the first resolution date (Jan 25), the 30-day window is almost entirely in the past and encompasses the known Jan 2026 surge (72%). 
For the second resolution date (Feb 17), the window lies slightly in the future, so uncertainty dictates a prediction closer to 0.5 (63%), as a government crackdown or temporary cessation of gang conflict could bring the monthly count down toward or below the average.
